concept: Twilight goat amphitheater beneath a cosmic star-crest storm
scene: An asymmetric risograph poster set in a twilight desert-farm amphitheater where a slanted fence line climbs diagonally from the lower left to the upper right, guiding the eye from a cluster of bold goat silhouettes toward a distant stage glow. In the lower left, a torn cassette-card shape becomes a weathered gate panel half-buried in sand, its folded seam and ragged edge rendered as chunky cream paper forms. Stepping across it, a large white goat leads several smaller brown goats uphill through generous off-white negative space, their bodies simplified into flat shapes with visible grain. Midway up the slope, a rectangular teal spotlight frame encloses an adult singer in a floral jacket, reduced to a strong microphone-holding silhouette, while behind and above the figure a mirrored pair of elegant profile faces emerge from the darkness like mountain ridges or carved rock formations, their curls blending into a symmetrical mask-like cliff. Cutting across the sky, a huge black star tilts into the composition, echoed by a ring of tiny stars that orbit like a badge or halo. At the upper right, the night breaks into an abstract orange-and-teal splash cloud, as if a cosmic rainbow storm is spilling over the scene in speckled riso droplets. Perched within that storm is a reimagined eagle crest, its wings spread as a printed emblem on a banner or moon, clutching an olive branch while the other talon holds only an abstract baton-like shape, with arrows fanning upward into the sky. Everything is flattened into two-colour and overprinted forms on off-white paper, with slight misregistration, grainy textures, and bold silhouettes so the farm, stage, emblem, portraits, and explosion read as one strange shared world.
prompt: Create one bold risograph print - flat layered shapes, coarse riso grain, overlapping translucent inks slightly out of register, paper texture showing through. The image is a 16:9 music blog header. Scene: An asymmetric risograph poster set in a twilight desert-farm amphitheater where a slanted fence line climbs diagonally from the lower left to the upper right, guiding the eye from a cluster of bold goat silhouettes toward a distant stage glow. In the lower left, a torn cassette-card shape becomes a weathered gate panel half-buried in sand, its folded seam and ragged edge rendered as chunky cream paper forms. Stepping across it, a large white goat leads several smaller brown goats uphill through generous off-white negative space, their bodies simplified into flat shapes with visible grain. Midway up the slope, a rectangular teal spotlight frame encloses an adult singer in a floral jacket, reduced to a strong microphone-holding silhouette, while behind and above the figure a mirrored pair of elegant profile faces emerge from the darkness like mountain ridges or carved rock formations, their curls blending into a symmetrical mask-like cliff. Cutting across the sky, a huge black star tilts into the composition, echoed by a ring of tiny stars that orbit like a badge or halo. At the upper right, the night breaks into an abstract orange-and-teal splash cloud, as if a cosmic rainbow storm is spilling over the scene in speckled riso droplets. Perched within that storm is a reimagined eagle crest, its wings spread as a printed emblem on a banner or moon, clutching an olive branch while the other talon holds only an abstract baton-like shape, with arrows fanning upward into the sky. Everything is flattened into two-colour and overprinted forms on off-white paper, with slight misregistration, grainy textures, and bold silhouettes so the farm, stage, emblem, portraits, and explosion read as one strange shared world. Weave in recognisable elements taken from the 8 uploaded album covers: source 1: adult singer holding a microphone in a floral jacket; source 2: large black five-pointed star; source 3: explosive orange-and-blue abstract splash cloud; source 4: torn folded cassette insert shape; source 5: mirrored adult female profile faces forming a symmetrical mask-like center; source 6: large white goat with smaller goats around it; source 7: teal rectangular studio-frame portrait space; source 8: wing-spread eagle inside a circular ring of stars. Transform the subjects, figures, creatures, objects, and symbols depicted in the uploaded covers so they stay recognisable, and place them all inside one cohesive world with a single shared setting and consistent light. Redraw each album motif as bold flat printed shapes in the limited ink colours - simplified but instantly recognisable silhouettes and forms. Composition: An asymmetric poster composition with one strong diagonal flow, motifs at varied scales, and generous negative space. Colour direction: black, fluorescent orange, teal blue - strictly two or three ink colours pulled from the sleeves, overprinting where they overlap, on off-white paper. Mood: mythic, nocturnal, playful, and slightly surreal. Keep the scene safe and tasteful: feature only adults (no children, infants, or minors), and show no gore, wounds, body horror, medical or anatomical imagery, blank or milky eyes, nudity, or sexual content. Reinterpret any such source motif abstractly, and suggest rather than exactly replicate the likeness of any identifiable real person. Do not include any text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, or signage anywhere in the image. Avoid: readable text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, signage, grid layout, contact sheet, evenly tiled squares, raw album-cover thumbnails, collage of separate panels, photorealistic photograph, DSLR photo, 3D render, CGI, octane render, glossy 3D rendering, camera depth of field, lens bokeh, realistic skin texture, a posed group of people arranged for the camera, objects displayed on tables, trays, or plinths, one giant central sculpture as the centrepiece of the scene. Bold, confident, gallery-quality print design with a strong sense of place.
